title: 'Will resign in an hour': Abhishek Banerjee challenges TMC rebels to return to Mamata
content: Abhishek Banerjee challenged former Trinamool Congress colleagues to return to Mamata Banerjee. He stated they would not return as they made a deal with the BJP. Banerjee claimed these individuals received protection from central agencies after switching sides. He mentioned facing multiple summons himself but never running away. This challenge comes amid the Trinamool Congress's significant organizational crisis.

title: Watch: Farmers throw chilli powder at Telangana police in protest against land fencing
content: Protests broke out in Hyderabad's Shamshabad on Saturday as farmers opposed the fencing of 650 acres earmarked for a proposed high-speed train hub. The demonstration turned tense after protesters allegedly hurled stones and chilli powder at police personnel, prompting detentions. Farmers claimed they had cultivated the land for years, while officials maintained that the parcel belongs to the government.

title: With retirement rumours swirling, Rohit turns up for optional practice at Lord's
content: With retirement speculation growing and pressure mounting after a lean ODI series, Rohit Sharma attended India's optional practice session at Lord's. Former India captain looked in good touch as he prepared ahead of the series decider at Lord’s.

title: US govt considering adding $100,000 bond to Green Card applications for those seeking ...
content: The US government is considering a $100,000 bond for Green Card applicants. This measure aims to ensure immigrants are financially self-sufficient and do not burden public programs. The Trump administration is exploring this long-standing legal authority under the Immigration and Nationality Act. This proposal follows earlier attempts to impose fees on H-1B visa applications. Officials stated this policy reduced overstays in pilot programs.

title: India rolls out 'White Rabbit' tech to secure Indian time network: What it means
content: India has launched a secure, indigenous time signal network for critical infrastructure. This initiative reduces reliance on foreign timing sources for essential services. The system uses White Rabbit technology for highly accurate time synchronization. It aims to enhance reliability in banking, telecom, and power grid operations. This development supports India's technological self-reliance and digital economy.

title: Iran Supreme Leader's message to US: Mojtaba Khamenei calls Trump's signature worthless
content: Iran's Supreme Leader warned the United States of "unforgettable lessons" if attacks continued. He declared President Trump's signature "worthless and invalid" amid escalating conflict. Tehran suspended commitments under a recent interim agreement, citing US breaches. Both nations exchanged strikes on military and infrastructure targets. Regional security and global energy supplies remain a concern.

title: Donkeys fed 'gulab jamuns' in ritual amid rainfall deficit in 35 MP districts
content: Donkeys were fed gulab jamuns in Bhopal as part of a ritual seeking rainfall. Many districts in Madhya Pradesh have recorded below-normal precipitation this monsoon season. Participants offered prayers to Indra, the Hindu god of rain, for good showers. Bhopal has received above-normal rainfall, while other areas face deficits. A wet spell is anticipated across Madhya Pradesh starting Sunday.

title: Airbnb CEO says will keep investing in New York despite Mamdani's rent freeze plan
content: Airbnb and Anthropic are expanding their New York City operations significantly.  This investment occurs despite concerns from business leaders about city policies.  Airbnb purchased a building for its growing New York workforce.  Anthropic will lease a large office building and double its staff.  These expansions highlight continued business activity amidst policy debates.

title: 'Happened so fast that ... ': How police took away Sonam Wangchuk in 5 minutes
content: Eyewitnesses described Sonam Wangchuk's swift removal from Jantar Mantar by police.  White bedsheets shielded the operation, preventing public view of the activist's departure.  Police stated Wangchuk was taken to hospital following medical advice and court orders.  Protesters alleged the police action was timed to minimize visibility and support.  Despite the removal, demonstrators vowed to continue their protest for educational reforms.

title: How 58 beavers revived a valley buried by Mount St. Helens eruption
content: More than 40 years after the Mount St. Helens eruption buried a Washington river valley under volcanic sediment, 58 relocated beavers have helped restore the damaged landscape. Their dams and wetlands brought back native plants, fish and wildlife. However, conservationists fear a planned government project to raise a sediment retention structure could undo years of natural restoration.

title: Human voices near salmon streams made bears and eagles flee, study finds
content: New findings from Canadian researchers reveal that human sounds near Alaska's salmon streams significantly disturb wildlife, leading to grizzly bears avoiding feeding areas. This interference disrupts the natural nutrient transfer to surrounding forests, emphasizing the detrimental effects of human activity on essential ecosystem dynamics. Scientists propose enhanced land management practices to safeguard these critical ecological processes.

title: Scientists fit 75 pronghorns with GPS collars: What they found
content: GPS collars track pronghorn movement near a New Mexico solar project.  Fences around the solar farm present a significant barrier to these animals.  Pronghorn typically crawl under fences rather than jumping over them.  Researchers suggest simple fence modifications could aid wildlife movement.  These changes would help animals use their traditional migration paths.

title: Loose-lipped, drunken bar confession exposes murder of 16-year-old eloped girl
content: A week-long mystery ended with the exhumation of a 16-year-old girl's body.  The accused minor boy's uncle confessed to the crime while intoxicated.  The teenagers had eloped from Bengaluru and were living as daily wage earners.  An argument led to an accidental discharge of a gun, causing the girl's death.  Police have taken the accused into custody and are investigating the incident.

title: Argentina on the brink of unique World Cup record as Messi eyes another title
content: Argentina are one goal away from setting a FIFA World Cup record for most goals scored from outside the box in a single tournament, having already matched the mark with five. Lionel Messi has scored two and holds the individual record. Defending champions Argentina face Spain in Sunday's final, with both the title and a historic milestone at stake.

title: High-voltage power line crashes onto Delhi-Jaipur highway, traffic hit for over 2 hours
content: A snapped high-voltage power line caused a significant traffic standstill on NH-48.  The incident near Gurugram halted vehicles for over two hours on Saturday.  Authorities rerouted traffic, causing extensive delays and long queues for commuters.  Electricity department teams worked for two hours to clear the fallen transmission line.  Traffic movement gradually resumed after the area was declared safe for travel.

title: Maple Leafs' Gavin McKenna trains with Sharks star Macklin Celebrini
content: Toronto Maple Leafs’ prospect Gavin McKenna was recently spotted in a competitive offseason skate in Vancouver with some of hockey's brightest young talent. The training session gave fans a glimpse of the next generation of the NHL with San Jose Sharks forward Macklin Celebrini, Chicago Blackhawks prospect Michael Misa and San Jose Sharks defenseman Sam Dickinson.

title: NFL trade rumor: Patriots' $4mn star linked to Commanders, Falcons
content: The New England Patriots’ star player Kayshon Boutte caught 33 passes for 551 yards and 6 touchdowns across 14 games in the last season, sparking chaos among many. While his performance has been pretty impressive, with the New England Patriots acquiring A.J. Brown and signing Romeo Doubs, Kayshon Boutte has found his name amid trade speculations. Amid all the mess, the Washington Commanders and the Atlanta Falcons have emerged as potential landing spots for him.

title: Former NHL star Mike Commodore warns Oilers parents at development camp
content: Ex-NHL defenseman Mike Commodore has escalated his years-long war on the Edmonton Oilers by issuing a warning to parents attending the club’s development camp. The social media post went viral across the NHL, reigniting discussion of Commodore’s past with former coach Mike Babcock, and drawing mixed reactions from hockey fans and analysts.

title: Can a biopsy really make cancer spread? Oncologist explains why this myth puts lives at risk
content: A persistent myth in India wrongly claims biopsies spread cancer, causing significant fear. This misconception leads many families to postpone essential diagnostic tests for lumps. Delaying biopsies allows cancer to progress naturally, not due to the procedure itself. Experts emphasize that biopsies are safe and crucial for accurate cancer diagnosis. Early detection through biopsies significantly improves treatment outcomes and survival rates.

title: Amarnath, Vaishno Devi Yatras temporarily suspended due to bad weather
content: Authorities have temporarily suspended the Amarnath and Vaishno Devi Yatras due to inclement weather.  The decision was made as a precautionary measure to ensure the safety of all pilgrims.  Pilgrims are advised to remain patient and follow official communication channels for updates.  The Shri Amarnathji Yatra will remain suspended from both Pahalgam and Baltal routes.  The Yatra will resume once weather conditions are deemed safe for travel.

title: Watch: Rohit shares a laugh with Gambhir at Lord's balcony ahead of ODI decider
content: Rohit Sharma looked relaxed ahead of India's series-deciding third ODI against England, sharing a light-hearted moment with coach Gautam Gambhir during training at Lord's. With the series tied 1-1, India will bank on Virat Kohli's form and hope Rohit rediscovers his touch. A win would secure the series and India's first ODI victory at Lord's since 2004.

title: Third-Place Showdown: Why England vs France is a 900-year-old derby
content: Old enemies England and France meet in football’s most unwanted fixture, with Didier Deschamps’ farewell, the Golden Boot and 900 years of competitive irritation at stake. Read more in today's OFFSIDE.

title: 'Blood for blood': Iran displays giant billboard with Trump family in coffins
content: A new billboard in Tehran shows Donald Trump and his family above coffins draped in the US flag. The prominent slogan 'Blood for Blood' signals Iran's intent for revenge against recent strikes. This display follows other murals targeting Trump, including one showing him in an open coffin. These actions occur as military tensions between Iran and the United States intensify.

title: 'Shutting down Messi is impossible': Iniesta reveals what Spain must do vs Argentina
content: Andres Iniesta suggests Spain should not waste energy trying to stop Lionel Messi completely. He believes the key lies in Spain's own game and making Argentina uncomfortable. The former captain urged La Roja to play with confidence and trust their established style. Spain have remained unbeaten and conceded just once throughout the tournament. The team's collective understanding and defensive discipline have brought them to the final.

title: IRDAI launches comic book series to simplify life insurance for consumers
content: IRDAI chairman Ajay Seth unveiled a new comic book series to improve life insurance literacy.  This initiative aims to simplify complex insurance concepts for the general public.  The stories feature a young advisor explaining financial decisions and their long-term impact.  The launch supports IRDAI's "Insurance for All" vision for informed financial planning.  Industry data highlights a significant life insurance protection gap across India.

title: 'Don't wait for tragic outcome': Anna Hazare urges Centre to hold talks with Sonam
content: Anna Hazare urged the government to hold talks with Sonam Wangchuk. Wangchuk was hospitalized after his hunger strike entered its twenty-first day. Police removed him from Jantar Mantar amid heavy security measures. Opposition leaders condemned the government's actions and suppression of dissent. Wangchuk demanded the education minister's resignation over NEET examination issues.

title: 'No worries at all': Morkel backs Rohit despite scrutiny, urges India to fix flaws
content: Bowling coach Morne Morkel expressed confidence in Rohit Sharma's current form.  He acknowledged challenging conditions for opening batters throughout the series.  Morkel highlighted Rohit's experience and calming influence on the batting lineup.  India's batting unit needs to adapt quicker to Lord's conditions.  Breaking middle-over partnerships remains a key bowling concern for the team.

title: 'If he's going, he should ... ': Kapil Dev on Rohit Sharma's retirement talks
content: Kapil Dev has urged fans to celebrate Rohit Sharma's career amid speculation that Sunday's third ODI against England could be his final international match. The former India captain said retirement is inevitable and hoped Rohit signs off with a century. The retirement talk has intensified after Rohit's lean run in the ongoing England ODI series.

title: National Film Awards 2026: Yami Gautam, Kartik Aryan among major winners
content: The winners of the 72nd National Film Awards were announced by the Ministry of Information and Broadcasting in New Delhi on Saturday. One of the biggest highlights of this year's honours was Yami Gautam winning the Best Actress award for her powerful performance in Article 370. Sharing the Best Actor honour were Kartik Aaryan for his transformative performance in Chandu Champion and Mammootty for his critically acclaimed role in the Malayalam horror drama Bramayugam.

title: 'Would wake up at noon, hit gym by 5': Yogi's 'babua' dig at Akhilesh's UP CM tenure
content: Uttar Pradesh Chief Minister Yogi Adityanath criticized former CM Akhilesh Yadav's tenure. He alleged Yadav lacked public engagement and a development vision for the state. Adityanath highlighted the BJP government's welfare schemes and development projects. He contrasted current progress with the previous administration's perceived failures. The chief minister also accused the opposition of betraying farmers and compromising law and order.

title: 'Only if...': Mehbooba Mufti on NC's J&K statehood protest invite in Delhi
content: Mehbooba Mufti will join the National Conference protest for Jammu and Kashmir statehood.  She demands the agenda also include Article 370 restoration and prisoner release.  Mufti believes protesting solely for statehood legitimizes the revocation of special status.  She urged for an all-party meeting to address disempowering issues affecting people.  Restoring statehood requires sustained political effort and engagement from leaders.

title: 'Fast, unpredictable': What players, coaches said about New Jersey pitch before WC final
content: The hybrid grass pitch at the New York New Jersey Stadium is a major talking point. Players and coaches have shared contrasting views on its speed and firmness. Rain significantly alters the playing surface, making it faster and smoother for matches. FIFA has managed the pitch carefully, allowing it time to recover. The final outcome may depend on Saturday's weather and its impact.

title: Confusion hindered Venezuela's response to earthquake that killed over 5,000: Report
content: Venezuelan military response to earthquakes faced significant delays. Poor coordination and delayed deployment orders slowed rescue efforts. Essential equipment shortages further hampered the armed forces' capabilities. Civilians and international teams performed initial rescue work. Economic crisis years eroded the military's operational readiness.

title: India's power sector set for strong FY27 growth on rising demand, capacity additions
content: India's power sector anticipates sustained growth through fiscal year twenty twenty-seven. Surging electricity demand and record peak consumption fuel this positive outlook. Continued capacity additions, especially renewables, will strengthen utility performance. Thermal power remains central, providing dependable base-load electricity generation. Energy security and storage development underpin the sector's long-term expansion.

title: Farm loans with less paperwork? Centre explores digital land record link
content: Digital land records may soon link with Unified Lending Interface for faster farm loans. This integration aims to provide secure, consent-based access to institutional credit for rural citizens. Officials discussed using authenticated digital land records to simplify formal credit access for farmers. The initiative seeks to speed up loan disbursal and lower transaction costs significantly. This plan will promote financial inclusion by using land records as trusted digital assets.

title: 2 held in Guwahati for painting Sonam Wangchuk murals on public wall
content: Two individuals were apprehended for painting activist Sonam Wangchuk murals on public walls.  Guwahati police registered two separate cases against the accused individuals.  Meanwhile, Sonam Wangchuk was shifted to Safdarjung Hospital after his hunger strike.  He completed a twenty-day fast demanding the education minister's resignation.  Police action drew criticism from several opposition parties and student organizations.

title: AIBE XXI result declared: Check direct link to download scorecards
content: The Bar Council of India has announced the AIBE XXI Result 2026 on its official website. Candidates who appeared for the June 7 examination can now download their scorecards using their login credentials. The result is crucial for obtaining the Certificate of Practice, while candidates are advised to verify all details on the scorecard and report any discrepancies promptly.

title: CWG 2026: Mirabai, Lovlina named India's flagbearers for Glasgow opening ceremony
content: Mirabai Chanu and Lovlina Borgohain will lead India's 124-member contingent. They will serve as flag and baton bearers at the opening ceremony. Both accomplished athletes are also strong medal contenders for India. The Indian Olympic Association announced their selection on Saturday. The Games opening ceremony is scheduled for July 23.

title: 'Affair, snakebite, Rs 20L insurance': How UP woman, lover plotted husband's murder
content: A woman and her lover were arrested for a planned murder to claim insurance money. They sedated the husband and released a poisonous snake in his blanket. The snakebite killed the man, and the wife initially claimed it was an accident. Investigators found evidence of repeated calls and pictures of a snake. Both confessed to orchestrating the crime with snake charmers for financial gain.

title: Mother, son complete their IIT-M programmes together
content: While Aditya Kapadia completed his BS degree programme in Data Science and Applications, his mother completed a Diploma in Data Science

title: Mines and Geology Department recommends referring Ballari mining representations to Justice Dhulia committee
content: Mines and Geology Department says it is not the competent authority to decide the issues raised in the representation

title: NC expels Ladakh leader Akhoon as he refuses to step down as CEC of Kargil hill council
content: The action follows Dr. Mohammad Jaffer Akhoon’s refusal to step down as Chief Executive Councillor of the Ladakh Autonomous Hill Development Council, failing to honour the NC-Congress 2023 pre-poll agreement for power sharing

title: Man held for smuggling ganja in government bus at Katpadi
content: After driving through Chittoor, the bus halted at the check post near Katpadi when a team of police personnel checked the vehicle and luggage of commuters

title: Annual sports day held in Walajah
content: As part of celebrations, track and field events, including relay race, long jump, high jump, javelin throw, discus throw, kabaddi, football, and volleyball were conducted for students

title: Parliament session, CJP's rally plan key reasons for Wangchuk's removal from protest site
content: Security agencies were also apprehensive of law and order situation in the national capital in view of CJP’s proposed rally

title: Minister inaugurates synthetic hockey turf, Kodai Vizha in Tiruvannamalai
content: The new hockey turf, which will be maintained by SDAT has a spectators gallery with a seating capacity of over 480 persons to watch hockey and athletics events

title: Stone laid for road development works worth ₹3.02 crore in Vijayawada
content: The projects, funded under the 15th Finance Commission’s Air Quality Improvement Programme, are aimed at improving urban infrastructure while reducing dust pollution

title: Uddhav Thackeray calls for ‘BJP-free’ Ram movement
content: Shiv Sena (UBT) chief criticises the BJP-led Union government over alleged financial misappropriation at the Ayodhya Ram temple and the police action on protesters at Jantar Mantar

title: Dr. Ambedkar Law University cancels affiliation for three private law colleges
content: The Syndicate of the University, in its meeting early this month, passed a resolution cancelling the affiliation given to these colleges in 2025-26

title: Five of family found dead in Raipur; police suspect murder-suicide
content: Preliminary investigations suggest the man poisoned his family members before ending his life, though the exact cause of death will be confirmed following a detailed post-mortem and forensic analysis

title: Congress accuses Nirmala Sitharaman of repeating ‘false claims’
content: The party leaders question the Union Finance Minister’s claims on employment and bank loans, and seek details of loan beneficiaries and recoveries from economic offenders

title: Sonam Wangchuk hunger strike LIVE: Wangchuk continues his fast in hospital; CJP's Dipke claims Delhi Police planning overnight 'crackdown' on protest
content: An independent expert from AIIMS has concurred with the treating team that Sonam Wangchuk needs "immediate medical intervention", hospital said in its latest bulletin

title: Aamir Khan receives alleged threat from Lawrence Bishnoi gang, police verifying social media post
content: No formal complaint has been lodged by the actor or his representatives regarding the alleged threat so far

title: Left parties condemn Wangchuk's removal, use of force; back ongoing protest
content: In the early hours of day 21 of the protest fast at Jantar Mantar, when the presence was a little thin, Delhi Police succeeded in removing Sonam Wangchuk from the protest site, said CPI(ML) Liberation general secretary Dipankar Bhattacharya

title: Industries Minister P.K. Kunhalikutty says new govt.  has taken ₹300-crore Tata project further
content: Mr. Kunhalikutty said the Tata representatives met the government to discuss the project’s implementation

title: ‘Wangchuk not alone’: At least 21 continue indefinite hunger strike
content: Three AISA activists enter 21st day of hunger strike; protesters have been advised by doctors to rest as much as possible, conserve energy and speak less

title: Panels examining Bill for removal of Ministers and VBSA Bill defer meeting on adopting draft reports
content: The decisions come just before the Monsoon Session of Parliament is to commence. Opposition calls it ‘big victory’, says the humiliation that Modi government suffered in the defeat of delimitation-related Bills in April ‘still lingers’

title: Lok Sabha Speaker approves merger of six Shiv Sena (UBT) MPs with the Eknath Shinde-led Shiv Sena
content: Rebel TMC leaders Sudip Bandopadhyay and Kakoli Ghosh Dastidar are invited for a meeting of floor leaders by Parliamentary Affairs Minister Kiren Rijiju

title: Amit Shah inspects indigenous border security technology in West Bengal
content: This three-day trip is his first visit to the State after the Suvendu Adhikari government took charge of West Bengal

title: Sonam Wangchuk forcibly hospitalised; CJP founder begins fast, vows to march to Parliament on July 20
content: Police cite Delhi High Court order after moving Mr. Wangchuk to Safdarjung Hospital, where he refused medical interventions; Opposition leaders say government is repressing peaceful dissent

title: NMC will no longer issue eligibility certificates to doctors seeking teaching posts in routine cases
content: More complicated cases must take the institutional route, with a fee of ₹25,000 plus tax; move aims to promote institutional accountability in faculty appointments and promotions, reduce admin burden on Board

title: Structures linked to Muslims targeted for electoral gains ahead of Assembly election, says Opposition in U.P.
content: Opposition parties request the Supreme Court to intervene as the custodian of the Constitution of India, entrusted to protect the fundamental rights of citizens from Executive overreach

title: How will U.S. visa policy changes affect students, media professionals, and exchange visitors | Explained
content: The U.S. Department of Homeland Security cites national security risks from Indian and Chinese students and introduces changes in F, J and I categories of visas. PhD scholars, journalists, and exchange visitors will be impacted

title: CBSE declares class 10 second board exam results
content: The results for the Class 10 Second Board Exams can be accessed by students through their DigiLocker Results Portal on results.digilocker.gov.in

title: Vikram-1, country’s first private orbital-class rocket, successfully places tech payloads, postcards into orbit
content: Dubbed ‘Mission Aagaman’ (meaning arrival), the successful launch of Skyroot Aerospace’s Vikram-1 marks India’s entry into an elite group of nations with private orbital launch capability

title: ISRO,SCTIMST sign pact for research in space medicine, Bioastronautics
content: SCTIMST and HSFC have signed an implementing agreement for initiating a PDF programme in Bioastronautics, which is the first programme of its kind in the country

title:
Bulk waste generators in A.P. told  
to set up on-site processing units
content: Entities generating over 100 kg of solid waste daily are required to register as Bulk Waste Generators on Central Pollution Control Board’s online portal

title: 2 US Soldiers Killed, 1 Missing After Iranian Strikes On Jordan
content: CENTCOM said it would not release further details, including the identities of the service members who died, until 24 hours after their relatives have been formally notified.

title: "Worthless, Devoid Of Credibility": Mojtaba Tears Into Trump Over Iran Deal
content: As renewed hostilities flared on Saturday, Iran's Supreme Leader Mojtaba Khamenei vowed to teach the United States, who he referred to as Satan, "unforgettable lessons".

title: US-Iran War News LIVE: Iran's Supreme Leader In A New Statement Warns Of 'Unforgettable Lessons'
content: US-Iran War News Live Updates: Iran claimed it destroyed "several US refuelling aircraft and fighter jets" and caused "serious damage to many more".

title: Telangana Government To Enact Stringent Anti-Adulteration Law
content: Revanth Reddy said that chemicals used to artificially ripen fruits, extend the shelf life of vegetables and leafy greens, and preserve milk were contributing to health disorders.

title: Centre Signals Recognition Of NCPI With Trinamool Rebels, Triggers Row
content: Kiren Rijiju wrote to Sudip Bandyopadhyay, referring to him as the leader of the NCPI in the Lok Sabha

title: 2 Robberies, 1 Chilling Pattern: Bihar Village Fears "Hair-Chopping" Gang
content: The incidents, which took place eight days apart, have fuelled talk of a so-called "Baal Katarva" or hair-chopping gang operating in the area.

title: 'Return To Mamata Banerjee, I'll Resign': Abhishek Banerjee's Dare To Rebels
content: Abhishek Banerjee was responding to a question on the ever-increasing defections from the TMC in the aftermath of its assembly poll debacle, leaving Mamata Banerjee hanging by a thread as far as her control over the party.

title: Tensions At NIT Silchar As Villagers Remove Locked Gate Over Road Closure
content: The gate, located on the road connecting Bora Khali Tea Estate and nearby villages with the NIT campus, was reportedly locked without prior notice.

title: KTR's "Gaddari Ka Ghar" Jibe At Rahul Gandhi's "Mohabbat Ki Dukaan" Remark
content: KTR said Rahul Gandhi had failed to honour commitments made to unemployed youth during his visit to Ashok Nagar in Hyderabad.

title: "Us, Blade, Your Throat": Iran Hardliners Turn On Leaders Amid Coup Claims
content: Hardliners argue that Iran's leaders are sidelining parliament, ignoring the supreme leader's instructions during negotiations with the US and weakening the country's revolutionary institutions.

title: TDP Leader, 8 Family Members Strip Woman For Not Paying Bribe, Arrested
content: According to police, Murthy demanded a bribe of Rs 10,000 from a resident Nageshwar Rao on July 15. However, Rao declined to pay the bribe, leading to the attack on his wife.

title: 2 July 18s, 46 Years Apart, And Abdul Kalam Connections To Vikram-1 Launch
content: India's first indigenously developed satellite launch vehicle carried out its maiden successful mission on July 18, 1980.

title: Sonam Wangchuk's Fast, Ram Mandir Row To Dominate All-Party Meet Tomorrow
content: Additionally, the government has announced five new bills that will be introduced in the Lok Sabha or the Rajya Sabha during the upcoming Monsoon Session.

title: After NDTV Report, Centre Orders Probe Into B.Ed Colleges That May Not Exist
content: At the site linked to the college, NDTV found what appeared to be agricultural land, while nearby buildings bore the names of other institutions.

title: Karnataka To Celebrate Mysuru Dasara Amid Drought Fears, BJP Slams Congress
content: Celebrated every year in memory of the erstwhile royal family of Mysuru, the Dasara festival attracts lakhs of people from within and outside India.

title: Gujarat Tops NITI Aayog's Investment Friendliness Index 2026
content: The Investment Friendliness Index evaluates States across 84 indicators under eight key pillars, covering every stage of the investment lifecycle from policy and governance to infrastructure, business facilitation and fiscal management.

title: 'History Made': Gautam Adani Congratulates Skyroot For Vikram-1 Launch
content: In a post on social media platform X, Gautam Adani said the successful maiden orbital flight of Vikram-1, which achieved all its mission objectives, is a historic milestone for India's rapidly growing private space ecosystem.

title: Protests rock Dhaka after Hindu man arrested for building 81-ft Lord Ram statue

title: Nobody wants to wait on hold anymore. But can AI replace customer care?
content: Priyanshi Rastogi is a journalist with The Times of India. She primarily covers national and international news, along with business affairs. Her work focuses on the latest political events, in-depth explainers, features, offbeat articles and viral social media trending stories. title: How Skyroot gave India a private rocket to reach orbit
content: Chethan Kumar is a Senior Assistant Editor with the Times of India. Aside from specialising in Space & Science, he has reported extensively on varied topics, with special focus on defence, policy and data stories. He has covered multiple elections, too. As a young democracy grows out of adolescence, Chethan feels, there are reels of tales emerging which need to be captured. To do this, he alternates between the mundane goings-on of the Common Man and the wonder-filled worlds of scientists and scamsters, politicians and soldiers. In a career spanning nearly 18 years, he has reported from multiple datelines — Houston, Florida, Kochi, Hyderabad, Chennai, Sriharikota (AP), NH-1 (J&K Highway), New Delhi, Ahmedabad, Raichur, Bhatkal, Mysuru, Chamarajanagar, to name a few —  but is based out of Bengaluru, India’s science capital that also hosts the ISRO HQ. title: 'Netanyahu belongs in the Hague': Mamdani considering Israel PM's arrest during NYC visit
content: At TOI World Desk, our dedicated team of seasoned journalists and passionate writers tirelessly sifts through the vast tapestry of global events to bring you the latest news and diverse perspectives round the clock. With an unwavering commitment to accuracy, depth, and timeliness, we strive to keep you informed about the ever-evolving world, delivering a nuanced understanding of international affairs to our readers. Join us on a journey across continents as we unravel the stories that shape our interconnected world. Read More
